root_text.sn54.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kimilāyaṁ viharati veḷuvane.
root_text.sn54.10:1.3: Tatra kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ kimilaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn54.10:1.4: “kathaṁ bhāvito nu kho, kimila, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso”ti?
root_text.sn54.10:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā kimilo tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.sn54.10:2.3: tatiyampi kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ kimilaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn54.10:2.4: “kathaṁ bhāvito nu kho, kimila, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso”ti?
root_text.sn54.10:2.5: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā kimilo tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.sn54.10:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn54.10:3.3: Yaṁ bhagavā ānāpānassatisamādhiṁ bhāseyya, bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.sn54.10:4.2: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.sn54.10:4.4: “kathaṁ bhāvito ca, ānanda, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso?
root_text.sn54.10:4.5: Idhānanda, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn54.10:4.8: Evaṁ bhāvito kho, ānanda, ānāpānassatisamādhi evaṁ bahulīkato mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso.
root_text.sn54.10:5.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu dīghaṁ vā assasanto ‘dīghaṁ assasāmī’ti pajānāti, dīghaṁ vā passasanto ‘dīghaṁ passasāmī’ti pajānāti;
root_text.sn54.10:5.2: rassaṁ vā assasanto ‘rassaṁ assasāmī’ti pajānāti, rassaṁ vā passasanto ‘rassaṁ passasāmī’ti pajānāti;
root_text.sn54.10:5.4: ‘passambhayaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘passambhayaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—
root_text.sn54.10:5.5: kāye kāyānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:5.8: Tasmātihānanda, kāye kāyānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:6.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu ‘pītippaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘pītippaṭisaṁvedī passasissāmī’ti sikkhati;
root_text.sn54.10:6.5: vedanāsu vedanānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:6.7: Vedanāññatarāhaṁ, ānanda, etaṁ vadāmi, yadidaṁ—assāsapassāsānaṁ sādhukaṁ manasikāraṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:6.8: Tasmātihānanda, vedanāsu vedanānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:7.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu ‘cittappaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘cittappaṭisaṁvedī passasissāmī’ti sikkhati;
root_text.sn54.10:7.4: ‘vimocayaṁ cittaṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘vimocayaṁ cittaṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—
root_text.sn54.10:7.5: citte cittānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:7.7: Nāhaṁ, ānanda, muṭṭhassatissa asampajānassa ānāpānassatisamādhibhāvanaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.sn54.10:7.8: Tasmātihānanda, citte cittānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:8.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, ānanda, bhikkhu ‘aniccānupassī assasissāmī’ti sikkhati …pe…
root_text.sn54.10:8.5: dhammesu dhammānupassī, ānanda, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:8.6: So yaṁ taṁ hoti abhijjhādomanassānaṁ pahānaṁ taṁ paññāya disvā sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.
root_text.sn54.10:8.7: Tasmātihānanda, dhammesu dhammānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:9.2: Puratthimāya cepi disāyaṁ āgaccheyya sakaṭaṁ vā ratho vā, upahanateva taṁ paṁsupuñjaṁ;
root_text.sn54.10:9.5: dakkhiṇāya cepi disāya āgaccheyya sakaṭaṁ vā ratho vā, upahanateva taṁ paṁsupuñjaṁ.
root_text.sn54.10:9.6: Evameva kho, ānanda, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharantopi upahanateva pāpake akusale dhamme;
root_text.sn54.10:9.9: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharantopi upahanateva pāpake akusale dhamme”ti.

translation_text.sn54.10:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kimbilā in the Freshwater Mangrove Wood.
translation_text.sn54.10:1.3: Then the Buddha said to Venerable Kimbila,
translation_text.sn54.10:1.4: “Kimbila, how is immersion due to mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so that it is very fruitful and beneficial?”
translation_text.sn54.10:2.1: When he said this, Kimbila kept silent.
translation_text.sn54.10:2.3: And for a third time, the Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn54.10:2.4: “How is immersion due to mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so that it is very fruitful and beneficial?”
translation_text.sn54.10:2.5: And a second time and a third time Kimbila kept silent.
translation_text.sn54.10:3.1: When he said this, Venerable Ānanda said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn54.10:3.2: “Now is the time, Blessed One! Now is the time, Holy One!
translation_text.sn54.10:3.3: Let the Buddha speak on immersion due to mindfulness of breathing. The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.sn54.10:4.1: “Well then, Ānanda, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn54.10:4.4: “Ānanda, how is immersion due to mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so that it is very fruitful and beneficial?
translation_text.sn54.10:4.5: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.
translation_text.sn54.10:4.6: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …
translation_text.sn54.10:4.7: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.10:4.8: That’s how immersion due to mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated, is very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn54.10:5.1: There’s a time when a mendicant is breathing in heavily, and they know: ‘I’m breathing in heavily.’ Breathing out heavily they know: ‘I’m breathing out heavily.’
translation_text.sn54.10:5.2: When breathing in lightly they know: ‘I’m breathing in lightly.’ Breathing out lightly they know: ‘I’m breathing out lightly.’
translation_text.sn54.10:5.3: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing the whole body.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out experiencing the whole body.’
translation_text.sn54.10:5.4: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in stilling the physical process.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out stilling the physical process.’
translation_text.sn54.10:5.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:5.7: Because the breath is a certain aspect of the body, I say.
translation_text.sn54.10:5.8: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:6.1: There’s a time when a mendicant practices like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing rapture.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out experiencing rapture.’
translation_text.sn54.10:6.2: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing bliss.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out experiencing bliss.’
translation_text.sn54.10:6.3: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing the the mental process.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out experiencing the the mental process.’
translation_text.sn54.10:6.4: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in stilling the mental process.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out stilling the mental process.’
translation_text.sn54.10:6.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:6.7: Because careful application of mind to the in-breaths and out-breaths is a certain aspect of feelings, I say.
translation_text.sn54.10:6.8: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:7.1: There’s a time when a mendicant practices like this: ‘I’ll breathe in experiencing the mind.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out experiencing the mind.’
translation_text.sn54.10:7.2: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in gladdening the mind.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out gladdening the mind.’
translation_text.sn54.10:7.3: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in immersing the mind in samādhi.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out immersing the mind in samādhi.’
translation_text.sn54.10:7.4: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in freeing the mind.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out freeing the mind.’
translation_text.sn54.10:7.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:7.7: Because there is no development of immersion due to mindfulness of breathing for someone who is unmindful and lacks awareness, I say.
translation_text.sn54.10:7.8: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:8.1: There’s a time when a mendicant practices like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing impermanence.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing impermanence.’
translation_text.sn54.10:8.2: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing fading away.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing fading away.’
translation_text.sn54.10:8.3: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing cessation.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing cessation.’
translation_text.sn54.10:8.4: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.10:8.5: At such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:8.6: Having seen with wisdom the giving up of covetousness and displeasure, they watch closely over with equanimity.
translation_text.sn54.10:8.7: Therefore, at such a time a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn54.10:9.1: Suppose there was a large heap of sand at the crossroads.
translation_text.sn54.10:9.2: And a cart or chariot were to come by from the east,
translation_text.sn54.10:9.5: or south and destroy that heap of sand.
translation_text.sn54.10:9.6: In the same way, when a mendicant is meditating by observing an aspect of the body,
translation_text.sn54.10:9.9: or principles, they destroy bad, unskillful qualities.”
